title: ismene ringens
intro: Ismene ringens, also known as the Wild Jonquil or Amarillo, is a flowering plant native to South America. It is a perennial bulbous plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its funnel-shaped flowers and long, strap-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ismene ringens include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves to support skin and respiratory health.
